blurb: Cheerful, long-blooming prairie native with drooping yellow (sometimes mahogany-banded) petals around a tall sombrero-like cone — easy, drought-tough, and worked hard by bees all summer.
seasons: Yellow/red sombrero flowers early summer–fall; ferny gray-green foliage; dark seed cones into winter
wildlife: Bees and butterflies; seeds for birds
